# From Data Scientist to Production AI Lead {.unnumbered .visually-hidden}

<section class="book-hero">
  <h1>From Data Scientist to Production AI Lead</h1>
  <p>This book teaches industry data scientists how to move beyond notebooks and build real AI systems: models, APIs, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), identity, governance, agents, and delivery leadership.</p>
  <div class="book-hero__meta">
    <span class="book-pill">ML foundations</span>
    <span class="book-pill">LLM systems</span>
    <span class="book-pill">Backend APIs</span>
    <span class="book-pill">Security and governance</span>
    <span class="book-pill">Enterprise delivery</span>
  </div>
</section>

## The Reader Journey

The default flow is intentional:

1. Learn how models work and how to evaluate them.
2. Learn neural networks and transformer foundations.
3. Build Large Language Model (LLM) applications.
4. Wrap models and LLM workflows in backend services.
5. Add identity, access control, threat modelling, and governance.
6. Understand agentic systems and protocols.
7. Lead delivery from business pain to production rollout.
8. Finish with end-to-end projects.

In a notebook, you can optimize a model score. In production, you need a service contract, monitoring, and repeatable evaluation. In an enterprise, you must also prove who can access what, how decisions are reviewed, and how the system is supported after launch.

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A["Part 0<br/>Orientation"] --> B["Part I<br/>ML foundations"]
  B --> C["Part II<br/>Deep learning"]
  C --> D["Part III<br/>LLM applications"]
  D --> E["Part IV<br/>Backend services"]
  E --> F["Part V<br/>Security and governance"]
  F --> G["Part VI<br/>Agents and protocols"]
  G --> H["Part VII<br/>Enterprise delivery"]
  H --> I["Part VIII<br/>Projects"]
```

<section class="path-switcher" data-path-switcher>
<h2>Choose Your Reading Path</h2>
<div class="path-tabs" role="tablist" aria-label="Reading paths">
<input class="path-radio" type="radio" name="reading-path" id="path-ds" checked>
<label class="path-tab" for="path-ds">Data scientist</label>
<input class="path-radio" type="radio" name="reading-path" id="path-lead">
<label class="path-tab" for="path-lead">AI lead</label>
<input class="path-radio" type="radio" name="reading-path" id="path-backend">
<label class="path-tab" for="path-backend">Backend or ML engineer</label>
<input class="path-radio" type="radio" name="reading-path" id="path-interview">
<label class="path-tab" for="path-interview">Interview preparation</label>
</div>
<div class="path-panels">
<article class="path-panel" data-path-panel="ds">
<h3>Path A: Data scientist becoming production-ready</h3>
<ol>
<li>Start with Part 0 to understand the book and running case study.</li>
<li>Read Part I and Part II to strengthen ML and deep learning fundamentals.</li>
<li>Move through Part III and Part IV to build LLM applications and APIs.</li>
<li>Use the projects to turn the concepts into runnable systems.</li>
</ol>
</article>
<article class="path-panel" data-path-panel="lead">
<h3>Path B: AI lead or senior data scientist</h3>
<ol>
<li>Start with Part 0, then jump to Part III for modern LLM application trade-offs.</li>
<li>Read Part V and Part VI for security, governance, agents, MCP, and A2A.</li>
<li>Use Part VII as your delivery playbook for intake, architecture, rollout, and support.</li>
</ol>
</article>
<article class="path-panel" data-path-panel="backend">
<h3>Path C: Backend or ML engineer</h3>
<ol>
<li>Read Part IV first to align on APIs, FastAPI, jobs, Docker, and observability.</li>
<li>Read Part V for enterprise identity, tenant isolation, and AI security.</li>
<li>Read Part III and Part VI for RAG, tool calling, agents, MCP, and framework choices.</li>
</ol>
</article>
<article class="path-panel" data-path-panel="interview">
<h3>Path D: Interview preparation</h3>
<ol>
<li>Review ML foundations, deep learning, LLM applications, and backend basics.</li>
<li>Use the glossary, acronyms, checklists, and interview appendix to tighten explanations.</li>
<li>Practice explaining the enterprise document Q&A assistant end to end.</li>
</ol>
</article>
</div>
</section>

## Running Case Study

<section class="visual-band">
  <div>
    <h3>Enterprise Document Q&A Assistant</h3>
    <p>Employees waste time searching policies, architecture notes, project documents, meeting summaries, and operational procedures. The organization wants a secure assistant that answers questions using internal documents while respecting document-level access control.</p>
    <p>The case study follows one system across the book: ingestion, chunking, embeddings, retrieval, reranking, answer generation, citations, authentication, audit logs, evaluation, monitoring, and rollout.</p>
  </div>
  <img src="assets/generated/enterprise/document-qa-architecture.svg" alt="Enterprise document Q&A assistant architecture showing documents, ingestion, retrieval, LLM answer generation, and audit logging.">
</section>

## Parts

::: {.book-grid}

::: {.book-card}
<span class="part-kicker">Part 0</span>

### [Orientation](00-orientation/index.qmd)

Audience, delivery map, running case study, and how to use the book.
:::

::: {.book-card}
<span class="part-kicker">Part I</span>

### [ML Foundations](01-ml-foundations/index.qmd)

Regression, classification, ensembles, evaluation, leakage, feature engineering, and data contracts.
:::

::: {.book-card}
<span class="part-kicker">Part II</span>

### [Deep Learning](02-deep-learning/index.qmd)

Neural networks, PyTorch training, debugging, convolution, sequence models, transformers, and embeddings.
:::

::: {.book-card}
<span class="part-kicker">Part III</span>

### [LLM Applications](03-llm-applications/index.qmd)

Prompting, structured outputs, tool calling, RAG, evaluation, fine-tuning, alignment, cost, and latency.
:::

::: {.book-card}
<span class="part-kicker">Part IV</span>

### [Backend Engineering](04-backend-engineering/index.qmd)

HTTP, FastAPI, inference APIs, background jobs, concurrency, Docker, deployment, and observability.
:::

::: {.book-card}
<span class="part-kicker">Part V</span>

### [Security and Governance](05-security-governance/index.qmd)

Single Sign-On (SSO), OAuth2, OpenID Connect (OIDC), JSON Web Tokens (JWTs), tenant isolation, AI risk, and secure development.
:::

::: {.book-card}
<span class="part-kicker">Part VI</span>

### [Agentic Systems](06-agentic-systems/index.qmd)

Agents, workflows, tool safety, Model Context Protocol (MCP), Agent-to-Agent (A2A), frameworks, evaluation, and observability.
:::

::: {.book-card}
<span class="part-kicker">Part VII</span>

### [Enterprise Delivery](07-enterprise-delivery/index.qmd)

Intake, response documents, kickoff, first seven days, architecture, service selection, review, rollout, and support.
:::

::: {.book-card}
<span class="part-kicker">Part VIII</span>

### [Projects](08-projects/index.qmd)

Churn API, document Q&A assistant, agentic ticket triage, and forecasting service with monitoring.
:::

:::

## What Changed From the Legacy Static Site

The legacy static HTML chapters remain in the repository for reference. The maintainable book source now lives in Quarto so the table of contents, search, cross-page navigation, generated assets, examples, and CI checks can be managed consistently.
